title: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken

seo.title: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken Recipe | Crispy & Spicy
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken at home. Our easy recipe delivers crispy, sweet, and spicy perfection. Get cooking now!

seo.imageAlt: A plate of crispy Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken coated in sweet and spicy sauce, garnished with sesame seeds and green onions.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the perfect balance of sweet and spicy with our homemade Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken!

description: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a beloved dish featuring perfectly crispy chicken coated in a luscious, sticky, sweet, and spicy sauce. Each bite offers an irresistible crunch followed by a burst of complex flavors, making it a true culinary delight. This iconic Korean comfort food is perfect for sharing or a satisfying meal.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Pat the chicken pieces thoroughly dry with paper towels. Season lightly with salt and black pepper. In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, cornstarch, egg, and chilled sparkling water until a smooth batter forms. Add the chicken pieces to the batter, ensuring each piece is fully coated.

instructions.title: First Fry
instructions.text: Heat the vegetable oil in a large deep pot or Dutch oven to 350F (175C). Carefully add the battered chicken pieces, frying in batches to avoid overcrowding the pot. Fry for 6-8 minutes, or until light golden brown and cooked through. Remove the chicken with a slotted spoon and place on a wire rack to drain excess oil.

instructions.title: Prepare the Yangnyeom Sauce
instructions.text: While the chicken is frying, prepare the sauce. In a small saucepan, combine gochujang, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, corn syrup (or honey), minced garlic, and grated ginger. Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat, stirring constantly until the sugar dissolves and the sauce thickens slightly, about 3-5 minutes. Set aside.

instructions.title: Second Fry for Extra Crispiness
instructions.text: Increase the oil temperature to 375F (190C). Carefully return the partially fried chicken to the hot oil for a second fry. Fry for another 3-4 minutes, or until the chicken is deep golden brown and exceptionally crispy. This double-frying method is key to achieving the signature Korean fried chicken crunch.

instructions.title: Coat the Chicken with Sauce
instructions.text: Once the chicken is done with the second fry, immediately transfer it to a large mixing bowl. Pour the prepared Yangnyeom sauce over the hot chicken. Toss gently but quickly until all the chicken pieces are evenly coated with the sticky, flavorful sauce.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Transfer the sauced chicken to a serving platter. Garnish generously with toasted sesame seeds and chopped green onions. Serve immediately while the chicken is still warm and crispy. This dish is best enjoyed fresh.

tips: For the crispiest chicken, ensure your oil temperature is accurate. Use a cooking thermometer to monitor it during both frying stages.
Do not overcrowd the pot when frying chicken. Frying in smaller batches helps maintain oil temperature and ensures even cooking and crispiness.
The Yangnyeom sauce can be adjusted to your preference. Add more gochujang for extra spice or more corn syrup for a sweeter glaze.
article:
Get ready to embark on a culinary journey to the vibrant streets of Seoul, right from your own kitchen. Few dishes capture the heart and palate quite like Korean Fried Chicken, and among its many delectable variations, Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken stands out as a true icon. Famous for its impossibly crispy exterior, tender, juicy interior, and a sticky, glossy sauce that perfectly balances sweet, spicy, and savory notes, this dish is more than just food – it's an experience. If you’ve ever craved that authentic, finger-licking goodness, you’re in the right place. We’re about to unlock the secrets to making the best Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken you’ve ever tasted, guiding you through every step to achieve restaurant-quality results at home.

## What is Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken?

At its core, Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a celebration of texture and flavor. The name "Yangnyeom" (양념) literally translates to "seasoned" or "sauced," referring to the signature sweet and spicy coating that elevates this dish beyond a simple fried chicken. Unlike its American counterpart, which often features a thicker, breaded crust, Korean fried chicken is renowned for its ultra-thin, almost translucent, crackly-crisp skin, achieved through a unique double-frying method. This intense crispiness acts as the perfect canvas for the vibrant Yangnyeom sauce. The sauce itself is a complex symphony, typically featuring gochujang (Korean chili paste) for heat, soy sauce for umami, sugar or corn syrup for sweetness, rice vinegar for tang, and a generous amount of garlic and often ginger for aromatic depth. It’s a harmonious blend that creates a truly addictive flavor profile, making Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken a globally beloved dish that’s both comforting and exciting.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting authentic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken requires a few key components. Here's what you'll need:

**For the Chicken:**
*   **Chicken:** 2-3 lbs chicken wings, drumsticks, or boneless thighs. Bone-in pieces are traditional, but boneless works for quicker cooking.
*   **Marinade (optional but recommended):** 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp minced ginger, 1 tsp minced garlic, 1/2 tsp black pepper, 1 tbsp mirin (rice wine).
*   **Coating:** 1 cup potato starch (highly recommended for superior crispiness) or cornstarch, 1/4 cup all-purpose flour (optional, for a slightly lighter coating).
*   **Frying Oil:** Enough neutral oil with a high smoke point (vegetable, canola, peanut) to deep-fry the chicken.

**For the Yangnyeom Sauce:**
*   1/4 cup gochujang (Korean chili paste)
*   2 tbsp soy sauce
*   2 tbsp rice vinegar
*   3-4 tbsp sugar (brown sugar, honey, or corn syrup for glossiness)
*   2 tbsp minced garlic
*   1 tsp minced ginger (optional)
*   1 tsp sesame oil
*   1 tbsp ketchup (optional, for added tang and umami)
*   1 tbsp gochugaru (Korean chili flakes, for extra heat/color, optional)
*   2-3 tbsp water or chicken stock (to thin the sauce)

**Garnish:** Toasted sesame seeds, chopped green onions.

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps meticulously to achieve the perfect Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken:

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Pat the chicken pieces thoroughly dry with paper towels. This is crucial for crispiness. If marinating, combine all marinade ingredients and toss with the chicken. Let sit for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours in the refrigerator. After marinating, pat dry again to remove excess moisture.
2.  **Coat the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the potato starch (and flour, if using). Add the chicken pieces and toss vigorously until each piece is evenly and completely coated. The coating should look dry and powdery.
3.  **First Fry:** Heat about 2-3 inches of frying oil in a large pot or deep fryer to 325°F (160°C). Carefully add the chicken pieces in batches, ensuring not to overcrowd the pot. Fry for 6-8 minutes, or until cooked through and lightly golden. Remove the chicken and place it on a wire rack set over a baking sheet to drain. Let it rest for 5-10 minutes.
4.  **Second Fry:** Increase the oil temperature to 375°F (190°C). Return the chicken to the hot oil for a second fry, again in batches. Fry for another 3-5 minutes, or until deeply golden brown and incredibly crispy. This double-frying step is essential for the signature crunch. Remove and drain on a clean wire rack.
5.  **Make the Yangnyeom Sauce:** While the chicken is frying, combine all Yangnyeom sauce ingredients (gochujang, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, garlic, ginger, sesame oil, ketchup, gochugaru, water/stock) in a small saucepan. Bring to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly until the sugar dissolves and the sauce thickens slightly to a glossy consistency, about 3-5 minutes.
6.  **Combine and Serve:** Immediately after the second fry, transfer the hot, crispy chicken to a large bowl. Pour the warm Yangnyeom sauce over the chicken and toss quickly and thoroughly until every piece is evenly coated. Garnish with toasted sesame seeds and chopped green onions. Serve hot!

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that legendary Korean fried chicken crispiness and flavor isn't difficult, but a few pro tips can make all the difference:

*   **Double Frying is Non-Negotiable:** This is the golden rule for authentic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken. The first fry cooks the chicken through, and the second, higher-temperature fry creates that shatteringly crisp, thin crust that holds up beautifully to the sauce.
*   **Pat Chicken Dry, Then Dry Again:** Moisture is the enemy of crispiness. Ensure your chicken is as dry as possible before coating and frying.
*   **Use Potato Starch:** While cornstarch works, potato starch is superior for creating that unique, light, and airy crispiness characteristic of Korean fried chicken.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Fryer:** Frying in small batches maintains the oil temperature, preventing the chicken from becoming soggy and ensuring even cooking and crisping.
*   **Toss Immediately:** Coat the chicken with the warm sauce as soon as it comes out of the second fry. The heat helps the sauce adhere better and creates that delightful glossy finish.
*   **Adjust Spice and Sweetness:** Taste the Yangnyeom sauce before tossing. Add more gochujang or gochugaru for extra heat, or more sugar/honey for increased sweetness, to suit your preference.

## Serving Suggestions

Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a versatile dish that can be enjoyed in many ways. Traditionally, it's often served as part of "Chimaek" culture – a portmanteau of chicken and maekju (beer). A cold beer is the perfect counterpoint to the rich, spicy, and sweet flavors of the chicken.

Other popular serving suggestions include:
*   **Pickled Radishes (Chicken Mu):** Small, sweet, and tangy pickled radishes are a classic accompaniment, offering a refreshing palate cleanser between bites of rich chicken.
*   **Coleslaw:** A simple, creamy coleslaw can also provide a nice contrast.
*   **Steamed Rice:** For a more substantial meal, serve with a side of fluffy white rice.
*   **Soju:** For those who prefer spirits, a shot of chilled soju is another popular pairing.
*   **As an Appetizer or Main:** It works equally well as a shareable appetizer for a gathering or a satisfying main course for dinner.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is perfect as is, you can certainly experiment with variations to suit different tastes or dietary needs:

*   **Spice Level:** Easily adjust the heat by increasing or decreasing the amount of gochujang and gochugaru in the sauce. For a milder version, you can even reduce the gochujang and add a bit more ketchup or honey.
*   **Sweetness:** Play with the type and amount of sweetener. Honey or corn syrup will give a glossier finish than granulated sugar.
*   **Garlic & Ginger:** Feel free to increase the amount of minced garlic and ginger in the sauce for a more pungent and aromatic flavor profile.
*   **Boneless Chicken:** For easier eating, use boneless, skinless chicken thighs cut into bite-sized pieces. The cooking time will be slightly reduced.
*   **Air Fryer/Oven Baked (Healthier Option):** While deep frying yields the best results, you can achieve a decent crispiness by air frying or oven baking the coated chicken. Just toss with a little oil before baking/air frying, then follow the double-cooking method. The texture won't be identical, but it's a good alternative.
*   **Vegetarian Alternatives:** Tofu or cauliflower florets can be coated and fried using the same method, then tossed in the Yangnyeom sauce for a delicious vegetarian option.
*   **Other Korean Fried Chicken Sauces:** If you enjoy the crispy chicken but want to try different flavors, explore other popular sauces like soy garlic (ganjang) or honey butter.

Mastering Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken at home is incredibly rewarding. With its irresistible crunch and that perfectly balanced sweet, spicy, and savory glaze, it’s a dish that guarantees to impress. So roll up your sleeves, gather your ingredients, and get ready to create a truly unforgettable meal that brings the vibrant flavors of Korea straight to your table. Enjoy every crispy, saucy bite!
